title: Kobani — Across the Line
introText:
January 2015. Kobani is not yet secured, but it is no longer contested.
A French TV producer and a cameraman. Two passports that won't be checked.
A camera, a tripod, audio gear. Body armour still in its plastic.
The border crossing is officially closed. You know three that aren't.
Engine on at 02:00. The last two kilometres before the border are black with ash.

beats.text:
The old crossing point is rubble now. YPG spotters wave you toward the second route without a word.
Three hundred metres of open ground. Then the tree line.

endings.text:
You get them across before first light. A YPJ escort takes them the rest of the way into the city.
Three weeks later, the segment airs on France 2 — eleven minutes, prime time.
The crossing point closes again two days after you used it.

The second route is watched tonight — a truck idles at the tree line with its lights off.
You turn back. The producer argues from the passenger seat the whole way to Erbil.
They file nothing from Kobani. The France 2 desk runs archive footage instead.
